text: The accesspoint shall provide a multimodal interface between users and the network.
The accesspoint shall enable speech controlled dialogs.
The system shall provide a special help system to address dead ends in dialogs.
The system shall provide contextual understanding during user interaction.
The system shall guide users throughout the interaction process to avoid cognitive overload.
The system shall maintain dialog status when switching between different modalities.
The software system shall support modular extensibility through component-based application interfaces.
The system shall detect people automatically without requiring a subject to approach the terminal.
The system shall provide speaker independent voice recognition for all users regardless of age or gender.
The system shall be implemented on a Microsoft Windows based platform.
The authoring tool shall provide a set of standard widgets including screen, dialog, text, button, slider, and picture.
The authoring tool shall manage a project repository containing font definitions, color palettes, bitmaps, and strings.
The authoring tool shall support the simulation of a user interface on a PC.
The authoring tool shall provide a menu editor to define items and navigation functions.
The authoring tool shall generate C code for a target platform.
The Vetronics system shall display video from several types of video sources without artifacts.
The system shall support textual and graphic overlay on displayed video for navigation and diagnostics.
The firmware shall include a UI-interpreter to visualize UI code generated by the UI Editor.
The firmware shall control all available hardware and expose hardware state to the UI-Code.
The UI Editor shall download the developed UI to the Vetronics hardware via Serial, USB, FireWire, IrDa, CAN, or other interfaces.
The UI Editor shall allow the connection of real actions to UI elements to define system behavior.
The UI Editor shall utilize a component-based architecture using COM or COM+ technologies.
The intelligent flight deck shall detect human slips and mistakes by comparing pilot intentions with actual flight status.
The system shall propose optimized resolution alternatives to the pilot during critical situations.
The system shall automatically adapt the Navigation Display range to show the cause of warning signals.
The user interface shall provide an option for experts to overrule the automation and adaptation.
The system shall incorporate auditory warning signals to augment the visual channel.
The intelligent flight deck system shall be programmed in Java using JACK Intelligent Agents.
